evidence.title: Zongertinib in Previously Treated HER2-Mutant Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer.
evidence.journal: The New England journal of medicine
evidence.populationHint: China/Chinese cohort or population mentioned in PubMed retrieval query
evidence.summary: Innovative oral targeted therapies are warranted for patients with human epidermal growth factor receptor 2 (HER2)-mutant non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Zongertinib is an oral, irreversible, HER2-selective tyrosine kinase inhibitor that has been shown to have efficacy in persons with advanced or metastatic solid tumors with HER2 alterations in a phase 1

evidence.title: Amivantamab plus lazertinib versus osimertinib in first-line EGFR-mutant advanced non-small-cell lung cancer with biomarkers of high-risk disease: a secondary analysis from MARIPOSA.
evidence.journal: Annals of oncology : official journal of the European Society for Medical Oncology
evidence.populationHint: China/Chinese cohort or population mentioned in PubMed retrieval query
evidence.summary: Amivantamab-lazertinib significantly prolonged progression-free survival (PFS) versus osimertinib in patients with epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR)-mutant advanced non-small-cell lung cancer [NSCLC; hazard ratio (HR) 0.70; P < 0.001], including those with a history of brain metastases (HR 0.69). evidence.title: The emerging roles of CEACAM6 in human cancer (Review).

evidence.summary: Carcinoembryonic antigen (CEA)‑related cell adhesion molecule 6 (CEACAM6) is a cell adhesion protein of the CEA family of glycosyl phosphatidyl inositol anchored cell surface glycoproteins. evidence.title: Amivantamab in EGFR Exon 20 Insertion-Mutated Non-Small-Cell Lung Cancer Progressing on Platinum Chemotherapy: Initial Results From the CHRYSALIS Phase I Study.
evidence.journal: Journal of clinical oncology : official journal of the American Society of Clinical Oncology
evidence.populationHint: China/Chinese cohort or population mentioned in PubMed retrieval query
evidence.summary: Non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC) with epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) exon 20 insertion (Exon20ins) mutations exhibits inherent resistance to approved tyrosine kinase inhibitors. evidence.title: Single-cell transcriptome analysis revealed a suppressive tumor immune microenvironment in EGFR mutant lung adenocarcinoma.

evidence.summary: Immunotherapy is less effective in patients with epidermal growth factor receptor (EGFR) mutant non-small-cell lung cancer (NSCLC). Lower programmed cell death-ligand 1 (PD-L1) expression and tumor mutation burden (TMB) are reported to be the underlying mechanism.
